question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TOCTOU race between connection.isValid() returning true and the actual HikariCP pool being initialized, so that the importer starts ingesting record files against a transiently unavailable database, causing those records to be silently dropped and never exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition between the mirror node host and the database after connection.isValid() succeeds but before the HikariCP pool commits the first record batch, causing the importer to proceed past the startup gate with no valid connection and silently miss exporting those records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that connection.isValid() only checks TCP-level connectivity and not schema integrity, so that the importer connects to a database whose schema migration is incomplete, causing record inserts to fail silently and those records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the broad catch (Exception e) block that swallows all connection errors and retries indefinitely, so that authentication failures are indistinguishable from transient connectivity failures, causing the importer to eventually connect to a wrong or degraded database instance and export incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any schema version check inside waitForDatabase(), so that the importer successfully passes the startup gate against a database running an older schema version, causing record fields to be mapped incorrectly and exported with wrong data to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the jdbcUrl parameter is passed directly to DriverManager.getConnection() with no validation or sanitization, so that if the URL is derived from an environment variable the attacker can influence, the importer connects to an attacker-controlled database and exports fabricated or missing records to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the password != null check (line 32) that treats an empty string password as valid, so that a misconfigured deployment with an empty password string passes the startup gate against an unintended database instance, causing incorrect records to be exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS enforcement in the DriverManager.getConnection() call used during startup validation, performing a man-in-the-middle attack to intercept the startup probe and redirect the importer to a rogue database, causing missing or incorrect records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the System.nanoTime() + properties.getTimeout().toNanos() deadline calculation (line 25) to cause a long-type overflow when a very large timeout is configured, making the deadline wrap to a past value and causing waitForDatabase() to throw IllegalStateException immediately, preventing the importer from ever connecting and causing all records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Math.toIntExact(properties.getValidationTimeout().toSeconds()) call (line 45) to trigger an ArithmeticException when validationTimeout exceeds Integer.MAX_VALUE seconds, causing the importer startup to crash before any records are exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Uninterruptibles.sleepUninterruptibly(properties.getInterval()) call (line 59) which cannot be interrupted by thread interruption signals, so that a misconfigured large interval causes the importer startup to stall indefinitely, blocking all record exports to mirror nodes during the stall window?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the retry loop (lines 42-60) does not distinguish between authentication failures and transient network errors, so that a credential rotation event causes the importer to retry with stale credentials until timeout, then throw IllegalStateException and miss exporting all records produced during that window?

[File: 'FileName: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that connectTimeout and socketTimeout are set as string-valued JDBC properties (lines 36-39) with no driver-level enforcement guarantee, so that a slow-responding rogue database keeps the startup probe connection open past the intended timeout, delaying the importer startup and causing a gap in exported records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the connectTimeout being truncated to whole seconds via toSeconds() (line 37), losing sub-second precision, so that a database that responds in under one second is treated as timing out, causing unnecessary retries and a startup delay that results in a gap of missing records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validation connection opened via DriverManager.getConnection() (line 43) is separate from the HikariCP pool connection, creating a TOCTOU window where the database becomes unavailable between the probe succeeding and the pool being created, causing the importer to start with a broken pool and miss exporting records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the log.info() call (lines 53-56) that logs the full exception message including potential database error details, to infer the database host, port, or schema structure from application logs exposed via a monitoring endpoint, then use that information to craft targeted attacks that cause incorrect records to be exported?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the IllegalStateException thrown at line 62 that embeds properties.getTimeout() in its message, leaking the configured startup timeout value, to time a database disruption attack precisely within the timeout window and cause the importer to fail startup and miss exporting records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that properties.isEnabled() returning false (line 20) causes waitForDatabase() to return immediately without any database check, so that if the enabled flag is set to false in a misconfigured deployment, the importer starts against an unavailable database and all records are missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DriverManager global JDBC driver registry to register a malicious JDBC driver that matches the jdbcUrl prefix before the legitimate driver, causing DriverManager.getConnection() to return a connection to an attacker-controlled endpoint that passes isValid() checks, leading to incorrect records being exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any database name or schema validation in the waitForDatabase() probe, so that the importer connects to the correct host but wrong database name (e.g., due to a URL misconfiguration), passes the isValid() check, and then exports records to the wrong schema, causing missing records in the intended mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that connection.isValid(timeout) (line 44) only sends a lightweight ping and does not verify write permissions for the importer user, so that the importer starts against a read-only replica instead of the primary, causing all record insert attempts to fail silently and records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retry loop that opens a new DriverManager connection on every iteration (line 43) without any connection rate limiting, to cause a connection exhaustion attack on the database by triggering rapid restarts of the importer, indirectly causing the database to reject legitimate connections and miss exporting records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the lastException (line 41) is overwritten on every retry iteration, so that the root cause of the first connection failure is lost and only the last exception is reported in the IllegalStateException, making it impossible to diagnose why records were missing from mirror node exports during the failure window?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any checksum or integrity verification of the database content inside waitForDatabase(), so that a database restored from a corrupted backup passes the isValid() probe and the importer exports incorrect or incomplete records to mirror nodes without detecting the corruption?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that socketTimeout is set as a JDBC property string (line 38) but its enforcement depends entirely on the JDBC driver implementation, so that a driver version that ignores socketTimeout allows a hung database connection to block the startup probe indefinitely, delaying the importer and causing a gap in exported records?
